title: Extension API
text: ntrd package designed extensible. ships built-data sources (CSV upload demo dataset), real power comes extension packages add new data sources — pulling data REDCap, institutional databases, system accessible via API. Extensions regular R packages : Declare ntrd extensions via DESCRIPTION field Define new data source class using S7 Implement three S7 generic methods: data_source_ui(), data_source_server(), data_load() Optionally provide additional UI, configuration persistence, scoring defaults ntrdWisconsin package full working example referenced throughout guide.

title: Extension API
text: ntrd Shiny app starts, calls discover_data_sources(), triggers following sequence: Scan: load_extensions() iterates installed packages (via .libPaths()) reads package’s DESCRIPTION file looking Config/ntrd/extension: true. Load: extension found, namespace loaded via loadNamespace(), triggers .onLoad() registers S7 methods. Introspect: discover_data_sources() inspects registered methods data_source_server data_source_ui generics, instantiates data source class, returns named list data_source objects. Render: Shiny app presents discovered data sources dropdown user choose .

title: Extension API
text: extension package must include two things DESCRIPTION: extension declaration field ntrd S7 imports Note field name exactly Config/ntrd/extension value must true (lowercase). relevant section ntrdWisconsin’s DESCRIPTION: package must call S7::methods_register() .onLoad() S7 method implementations registered namespace loaded: standard practice package defining S7 methods external generics.
code: # DESCRIPTION Package: myExtension Title: My ntrd Extension Version: 0.1.0 Imports:     ntrd,     S7,     shiny Config/ntrd/extension: true Imports:     S7,     ntrd,     ntrs,     shiny,     ... Config/ntrd/extension: true # R/zzz.R .onLoad <- function(...) {   S7::methods_register() }

title: Extension API
text: extension package must include two things DESCRIPTION: extension declaration field ntrd S7 imports Note field name exactly Config/ntrd/extension value must true (lowercase). relevant section ntrdWisconsin’s DESCRIPTION:
code: # DESCRIPTION Package: myExtension Title: My ntrd Extension Version: 0.1.0 Imports:     ntrd,     S7,     shiny Config/ntrd/extension: true Imports:     S7,     ntrd,     ntrs,     shiny,     ... Config/ntrd/extension: true

title: Extension API
text: package must call S7::methods_register() .onLoad() S7 method implementations registered namespace loaded: standard practice package defining S7 methods external generics.

title: Extension API
text: Use new_data_source() create S7 class inherits ntrd::data_source. provide three arguments: name: human-readable display name (shown data source dropdown) id: unique identifier string (used internally key) package: name package defining data source returns class (instance). Calling my_source() produces instance name id properties set. reference, ntrdWisconsin defines source class: Tip advanced use cases (e.g., adding custom S7 properties), can still use S7::new_class() directly parent = ntrd::data_source.
code: # R/my_source.R my_source <- ntrd::new_data_source(   name = "My Data Source",   id = "my_source",   package = "myExtension" ) # ntrdWisconsin/R/wadrc_source.R wadrc_source <- ntrd::new_data_source(   name = "Wisconsin ADRC",   id = "wadrc_redcap",   package = "ntrdWisconsin" )

title: Extension API
text: extension must provide S7 methods three generics exported ntrd. method file declare external generic using S7::new_external_generic(): Returns Shiny UI element (tagList) rendered user selects data source. ns argument namespace function — use wrap input IDs. Returns Shiny module server (via shiny::moduleServer()) must return list following elements: actual data retrieval happens. function receives data source object parameters $params() (unpacked via .call()). must return data_nacc object. data source doesn’t require user input (e.g., connects database preconfigured credentials, loads bundled dataset), generics simplify considerably. built-demo_source follows pattern: data_source_ui returns informational text instead input widgets data_source_server returns empty params list (reactive(list())) data_load takes source (extra arguments) works params() returns empty list, app calls data_load via .call(data_load, c(source = source, list())), resolves data_load(source).
code: data_source_ui <- S7::new_external_generic("ntrd", "data_source_ui", "source") data_source_server <- S7::new_external_generic("ntrd", "data_source_server", "source") data_load <- S7::new_external_generic("ntrd", "data_load", "source") S7::method(data_source_ui, my_source) <- function(source, ns) {   shiny::tagList(     shiny::passwordInput(       inputId = ns("api_token"),       label = "API Token"     )   ) } S7::method(data_source_server, my_source) <- function(source, id) {   shiny::moduleServer(id, function(input, output, session) {     params <- shiny::reactive({       shiny::req(input$api_token)       list(api_token = input$api_token)     })      restore <- function(params) {       shiny::updateTextInput(session, "api_token", value = params$api_token)     }      list(       params = params,       restore = restore,       session = session     )   }) } S7::method(data_load, my_source) <- function(source, api_token, ...) {   # Fetch data using your API   raw_data <- fetch_from_my_api(api_token)    # Transform to NACC format and validate   ntrd::data_nacc(data = raw_data) } S7::method(data_source_ui, my_source) <- function(source, ns) {   shiny::p("Click 'Go' to load data. No configuration needed.") }  S7::method(data_source_server, my_source) <- function(source, id) {   shiny::moduleServer(id, function(input, output, session) {     list(params = shiny::reactive(list()), session = session)   }) }  S7::method(data_load, my_source) <- function(source, ...) {   raw <- fetch_data_from_preconfigured_source()   ntrd::data_nacc(data = raw) }

title: Extension API
text: Returns Shiny UI element (tagList) rendered user selects data source. ns argument namespace function — use wrap input IDs.
code: S7::method(data_source_ui, my_source) <- function(source, ns) {   shiny::tagList(     shiny::passwordInput(       inputId = ns("api_token"),       label = "API Token"     )   ) }

title: Extension API
text: Returns Shiny module server (via shiny::moduleServer()) must return list following elements:
code: S7::method(data_source_server, my_source) <- function(source, id) {   shiny::moduleServer(id, function(input, output, session) {     params <- shiny::reactive({       shiny::req(input$api_token)       list(api_token = input$api_token)     })      restore <- function(params) {       shiny::updateTextInput(session, "api_token", value = params$api_token)     }      list(       params = params,       restore = restore,       session = session     )   }) }

title: Extension API
text: actual data retrieval happens. function receives data source object parameters $params() (unpacked via .call()). must return data_nacc object.
code: S7::method(data_load, my_source) <- function(source, api_token, ...) {   # Fetch data using your API   raw_data <- fetch_from_my_api(api_token)    # Transform to NACC format and validate   ntrd::data_nacc(data = raw_data) }

what: When no user configuration is needed
title: Extension API
text: data source doesn’t require user input (e.g., connects database preconfigured credentials, loads bundled dataset), generics simplify considerably. built-demo_source follows pattern: data_source_ui returns informational text instead input widgets data_source_server returns empty params list (reactive(list())) data_load takes source (extra arguments) works params() returns empty list, app calls data_load via .call(data_load, c(source = source, list())), resolves data_load(source).
code: S7::method(data_source_ui, my_source) <- function(source, ns) {   shiny::p("Click 'Go' to load data. No configuration needed.") }  S7::method(data_source_server, my_source) <- function(source, id) {   shiny::moduleServer(id, function(input, output, session) {     list(params = shiny::reactive(list()), session = session)   }) }  S7::method(data_load, my_source) <- function(source, ...) {   raw <- fetch_data_from_preconfigured_source()   ntrd::data_nacc(data = raw) }

title: Extension API
text: data sources must return data_nacc object data_load(). S7 class validates data construction, ensuring meets expected format. Either provide: VISITYR (numeric), VISITMO (numeric, 1–12), VISITDAY (numeric, 1–31) : VISITDATE (Date, "YYYY-MM-DD" format) VISITYR, VISITMO, VISITDAY provided, VISITDATE computed automatically year/month/day columns removed. NACCAGE auto-computed VISITDATE BIRTHYR/BIRTHMO already present. additional columns data (neuropsych scores, diagnostic variables, etc.) preserved can used app.

title: Extension API
text: Either provide: VISITYR (numeric), VISITMO (numeric, 1–12), VISITDAY (numeric, 1–31) : VISITDATE (Date, "YYYY-MM-DD" format) VISITYR, VISITMO, VISITDAY provided, VISITDATE computed automatically year/month/day columns removed.

title: Extension API
text: NACCAGE auto-computed VISITDATE BIRTHYR/BIRTHMO already present.

title: Extension API
text: additional columns data (neuropsych scores, diagnostic variables, etc.) preserved can used app.

title: Extension API
text: data_source_server returns $restore function, ntrd app offer users option save load configuration (e.g., API tokens). Configurations saved using safer package (encrypted disk) tools::R_user_dir("ntrd", "config") filename {source_id}.bin. $restore function receives named list $params() returns update Shiny inputs accordingly:

title: Extension API
text: Extensions can inject additional UI main ntrd app via $extras reactive values data_source_server. useful features go beyond data loading, displaying biomarker results. provide extension UI, include extension_ui extension_server extras reactive values: ntrd app : Call extras$extension_ui() render additional UI (currently “Biomarkers” tab) Call extras$extension_server(ptid, extras) selected participant ID can also pass arbitrary data extras (e.g., API tokens, cached query results) extension server can consume. ntrdWisconsin, mechanism used display biomarker data fetched Panda API:
code: extras <- shiny::reactiveValues(   extension_ui = function() my_extension_ui(id = "my-module"),   extension_server = function(ptid, extras) {     my_extension_server(id = "my-module", ptid, extras)   } ) extras <- shiny::reactiveValues(   all_values = NULL,   panda_api_token = NULL,   extension_ui = \() extension_ui(id = "biomarker-tables"),   extension_server = \(ptid, extras) {     extension_server(id = "biomarker-tables", ptid, extras)   } )

title: Extension API
text: data source selected user clicks “Go”, ntrd uses package property data source identify extension package checks whether defines .set_defaults() function. , called set standardization method defaults neuropsych scores. Define function package (need exported): Note ntrdWisconsin, .set_defaults() also called .onAttach() defaults set package attached directly ntrd app activates extension. Also, note T-scores referenced ntrdWisconsin found ntrsTscores package.
code: # R/zzz.R .set_defaults <- function() {   ntrs::set_std_defaults(ntrs::WAIS(), "tscores", overwrite = TRUE)   ntrs::set_std_defaults(ntrs::REY1REC(), "tscores", overwrite = TRUE)   # ... additional defaults }

title: Extension API
text: multiple extension packages define S7 generics std_using_* name, ntrd emit warning startup. Extension authors use shared generics common dependency (e.g., ntrs) rather redefining .

title: Extension API
text: summary key files ntrdWisconsin package map extension API: create new extension scratch, need minimum four files: R/my_source.R R/data_source_ui.R R/data_source_server.R R/data_load.R R/zzz.R Important Make sure source class file loaded method files. Use @include roxygen tags Collate field DESCRIPTION control file ordering.
code: my_source <- ntrd::new_data_source(name = "My Source", id = "my_source", package = "myExtension") data_source_ui <- S7::new_external_generic("ntrd", "data_source_ui", "source")  S7::method(data_source_ui, my_source) <- function(source, ns) {   shiny::tagList(     shiny::textInput(ns("token"), "API Token")   ) } data_source_server <- S7::new_external_generic("ntrd", "data_source_server", "source")  S7::method(data_source_server, my_source) <- function(source, id) {   shiny::moduleServer(id, function(input, output, session) {     params <- shiny::reactive({       shiny::req(input$token)       list(token = input$token)     })      list(       params = params,       session = session     )   }) } #' @include my_source.R data_load <- S7::new_external_generic("ntrd", "data_load", "source")  S7::method(data_load, my_source) <- function(source, token, ...) {   raw <- my_api_fetch(token)   ntrd::data_nacc(data = raw) } .onLoad <- function(...) {   S7::methods_register() }

title: Extension API
text: create new extension scratch, need minimum four files: R/my_source.R R/data_source_ui.R R/data_source_server.R R/data_load.R R/zzz.R Important Make sure source class file loaded method files. Use @include roxygen tags Collate field DESCRIPTION control file ordering.
code: my_source <- ntrd::new_data_source(name = "My Source", id = "my_source", package = "myExtension") data_source_ui <- S7::new_external_generic("ntrd", "data_source_ui", "source")  S7::method(data_source_ui, my_source) <- function(source, ns) {   shiny::tagList(     shiny::textInput(ns("token"), "API Token")   ) } data_source_server <- S7::new_external_generic("ntrd", "data_source_server", "source")  S7::method(data_source_server, my_source) <- function(source, id) {   shiny::moduleServer(id, function(input, output, session) {     params <- shiny::reactive({       shiny::req(input$token)       list(token = input$token)     })      list(       params = params,       session = session     )   }) } #' @include my_source.R data_load <- S7::new_external_generic("ntrd", "data_load", "source")  S7::method(data_load, my_source) <- function(source, token, ...) {   raw <- my_api_fetch(token)   ntrd::data_nacc(data = raw) } .onLoad <- function(...) {   S7::methods_register() }

title: Assessment Longitudinal Table — assessment_longitudinal_table
text: Create table raw scores across multiple visits colored corresponding standardized scores.

title: Assessment Longitudinal Table — assessment_longitudinal_table
code: assessment_longitudinal_table(   dat,   id = "NACCID",   date = "VISITDATE",   descriptions = c(Impaired = 0.03, Borderline = 0.1, `Low Average` = 0.26, Average =     0.76, `High Average` = 0.92, Superior = 0.97, `Very Superior` = 1),   fill_values,   methods = list(),   table_font_size = 100,   table_id = NULL,   show_all_visits = TRUE,   stubhead_label = NULL )

title: Assessment Longitudinal Table — assessment_longitudinal_table
text: dat data.table object. id character string specifying column data frame dat. Default "NACCID". date character string specifying column data frame dat. Default "VISITDATE". descriptions named numeric vector. Default names "Impaired", "Borderline", "Low Average", "Average", "High Average", "Superior", "Superior" corresponding values 0.03, 0.10, 0.26, 0.76, 0.92, 0.97, 1. fill_values Optional. methods Either list containing methods used standardization (character vector named entried method version), "infer" (default). "infer", methods pulled attributes standardized columns. table_font_size numeric value passed gt::tab_options(table.font.size = gt::pct(table_font_size)). Defaults 100. table_id Optional. ID given table. provided, random string assigned. show_all_visits boolean. TRUE (default), visits present data shown table. FALSE, visits least one standardized score presented. stubhead_label Optional. Passed gt::tab_stubhead(label = stubhead_label).

title: Assessment Longitudinal Table — assessment_longitudinal_table
text: shiny::HTML table created gt::gt object.

code: assessment_summary_table(summary_dat, bar_height = 16)  assessment_summary_data(   dat,   id = "NACCID",   descriptions = c(Impaired = 0.03, Borderline = 0.1, `Low Average` = 0.26, Average =     0.76, `High Average` = 0.92, Superior = 0.97, `Very Superior` = 1),   fill_values = NULL,   methods = "infer",   include_caption = TRUE )

title: Create NACC T-Cog Neuropsychological Assessment Summary Table — assessment_summary_table
text: summary_dat list returned assessment_summary_data(). bar_height pixels. Height percentile bars. Default: 16 dat Data use. Must exactly one row (data one participant one visit). id Column containing ID. descriptions named vector giving cutoffs use creating descriptions. fill_values (optional) named vector length (names) descriptions hex color values use. NULL, colorcoding used. default, evenly spread colors red yellow green used. methods (optional) either list named entries specifying model used standardizing cognitive scores, character string "infer". latter, methods infered dat object using NpsychBatteryNorms::methods_from_std_data. specified, footnotes added indicate methods used. include_caption Logical; caption included table age IQCODEs current visit?

NULL, colorcoding used. default, evenly spread colors red yellow green used. shade_descriptions logical; ranges descriptions shaded? fill_alpha opacity value shaded areas source passed plotly::plot_ly new_id Optional. String use plotly source ID. NULL (default), random string assigned.

title: NACC data class — data_nacc

Object construction fail provided data missing required columns, incorrect column types (NACCID must character; either VISITYR, VISITMO, VISITDAY numerics, VISITDATE "YYYY-MM-DD"; SEX EDUC must numeric; BIRTHYRand BIRTHMO NACCAGE must present numerics), contains invalid values (e.g., SEX 1/2/NA, VISITYR < 2005, VISITMO 1-12, VISITDAY 1-31, invalid date combination year/month/day).

code: longTableUI(id)  longTableServer(   id,   dat,   descriptions = c(Impaired = 0.03, Borderline = 0.1, `Low Average` = 0.26, Average =     0.76, `High Average` = 0.92, Superior = 0.97, `Very Superior` = 1),   fill_values = NULL,   methods = "infer",   table_font_size = 100,   print_updating = F )  longTableApp(   dat,   descriptions = c(Impaired = 0.03, Borderline = 0.1, `Low Average` = 0.26, Average =     0.76, `High Average` = 0.92, Superior = 0.97, `Very Superior` = 1),   fill_values = NULL,   methods = "infer",   table_font_size = 100,   print_updating = F )

title: Shiny Module to Display Longitudinal Trends in Table — longTableUI
text: id string tie UI Server dat data.table object. descriptions named numeric vector. Default names "Impaired", "Borderline", "Low Average", "Average", "High Average", "Superior", "Superior" corresponding values 0.03, 0.10, 0.26, 0.76, 0.92, 0.97, 1. fill_values Optional. methods Either list containing methods used standardization (character vector named entried method version), "infer" (default). "infer", methods pulled attributes standardized columns. table_font_size numeric value passed gt::tab_options(table.font.size = gt::pct(table_font_size)). Defaults 100. print_updating logical (default FALSE); message printed let user know table updating. debugging.

title: Shiny Module to Display Longitudinal Trends in Table — longTableUI

text: id ID string match module UI server UI dat Data use. Must exactly one row (data one participant one visit). descriptions named vector giving cutoffs use creating descriptions. fill_values (optional) named vector length (names) descriptions hex color values use. NULL, colorcoding used. default, evenly spread colors red yellow green used. methods (optional) either list named entries specifying model used standardizing cognitive scores, character string "infer". latter, methods infered dat object using NpsychBatteryNorms::methods_from_std_data. specified, footnotes added indicate methods used. table_font_size (optional) scalar indicating font size percentage. include_caption Logical; caption included table age IQCODEs current visit? print_updating logical (defualt FALSE); message displayed table updated? debugging. testing logical (default FALSE); app run testing mode?

title: Updated from gtExtras::gt_plt_bar_pct — my_gt_plt_bar_pct
text: updates gtExtras::gt_plt_bar_pct handle missing values desired, avoid reliance dplyr

title: Updated from gtExtras::gt_plt_bar_pct — my_gt_plt_bar_pct
code: my_gt_plt_bar_pct(   gt_object,   column,   height = 16,   width = 100,   fill = "purple",   background = "#e1e1e1",   scaled = FALSE,   labels = FALSE,   label_cutoff = 0.4,   decimals = 1,   font_style = "bold",   font_size = "10px" )

title: Updated from gtExtras::gt_plt_bar_pct — my_gt_plt_bar_pct
text: gt_object existing gt table object class gt_tbl column column wherein bar plot replace existing data. height number representing vertical height plot pixels. Defaults 16 px. width number representing horizontal width plot pixels. Defaults 100 px. Importantly, interacts label_cutoff argument, want change cutoff, may need adjust width well. fill character representing fill bar, defaults purple. Accepts named color (eg 'purple') hex color. background character representing background filling 100% mark bar, defaults light grey. Accepts named color (eg 'white') hex color. scaled TRUE/FALSE logical indicating value already scaled percent max (TRUE) needs scaled (FALSE). Defaults FALSE, meaning value divided max value column multiplied 100. labels TRUE/FALSE logical representing labels plotted. Defaults FALSE, meaning value labels plotted. label_cutoff number, 0 1, representing set inside/outside label boundary. Defaults 0.40 (40%) column's maximum value. value row less cutoff, label placed outside bar, otherwise placed within bar. interacts overall width bar, happy placement labels may try adjusting width argument well. decimals number representing many decimal places used label rounding. Defaults 1. font_style character representing font style labels. Accepts one 'bold' (default), 'italic', 'normal'. font_size character representing font size labels. Defaults '10px'.

title: Plot Module — plotUI
text: UI displaying plots standardized scores across visits. Server logic handle creation updating plots show standardized scores across visits. Shiny app using plotUI plotServer.

title: Plot Module — plotUI
code: plotUI(id)  plotServer(   id = "Attention/Processing",   dat,   x_range = c("2016-05-01", "2020-08-01"),   y_range = c(-2.5, 2.5),   descriptions = c(Impaired = 0.03, Borderline = 0.1, `Low Average` = 0.26, Average =     0.76, `High Average` = 0.92, Superior = 0.97, `Very Superior` = 1),   fill_values = calc_fill_colors(n = 7),   print_updating = T,   shade_descriptions = TRUE,   new_id = NULL )  plotApp(dat_input = prepare_data(demo_data), studyids = NULL, testing = FALSE)

title: Plot Module — plotUI
text: id single string. Optional, defaults "Attention/Processing". one nacc_var_groups. dat data.table. participant specific. x_range date range character vector two elements. y_range numeric vector two elements. Optional, defaults c(-2.5, 2.5). descriptions named numeric vector. Optional, defaults vector described function definition. fill_values vector fill colors. Optional, defaults calc_fill_colors(n = 7). print_updating logical value. Optional, defaults TRUE. shade_descriptions logical value indicating plots shaded according regions given descriptions colors given fill_values. Optional, defaults TRUE. new_id Optional. String used table ID. NULL (default), random string assigned. dat_input data frame. Defaults prepare_data(demo_data). studyids Optional. NULL (default), use unique values dat_input$NACCID. testing Logical, whether run app testing mode.

title: Plot Module — plotUI
text: shiny::tagList object containing two shiny::conditionalPanel's: one plot, one text standardized scores found. wrapped bslib::accordion_panel inclusion main app. return value. shiny app.

title: Create Labels for Assessment Summary Table — var_labels
text: CDRSUM Standard CDR sum boxes TRAILARR Number commission errors Trail Making Part TRAILALI Number correct lines Trail Making Part TRAILBRR Number commission errors Trail Making Part B TRAILBLI Number correct lines Trail Making Part B UDSBENRS Benson Complex Figure Recall - Recognized original stimulus among four options UDSBENTD Total score 10- 15-minute delayed drawing Benson figure UDSBENTC Total Score copy Benson figure REY1REC Rey AVLT Trail 1 total recall REY2REC Rey AVLT Trail 2 total recall REY3REC Rey AVLT Trail 3 total recall REY4REC Rey AVLT Trail 4 total recall REY5REC Rey AVLT Trail 5 total recall REYTCOR Rey AVLT delayed recognition; total correct REYFPOS Rey AVLT delayed recognition; total false positives CRAFTURS Craft Story 21 Recall (Immediate) - Total story units recalled, paraphrase scoring CRAFTVRS Craft Story 21 Recall (Immediate) - Total story units recalled, verbatim scoring CRAFTDVR Craft Story 21 Recall (Delayed) - Total story units recalled, verbatim scoring CRAFTDRE Craft Story 21 Recall (Delayed) - Total story units recalled, paraphrase scoring

24/24 CL) #>                                     OTRAILA                          OTRLARR #> 1 Oral Trailmaking Part A - Completion Time Oral Trailmaking Part A - Errors #>                      DIGFORCT                          DIGFORSL #> 1 Number Span Forward - Total Number Span Forward - Span Length #>                       DIGBACCT                           DIGBACLS #> 1 Number Span Backward - Total Number Span Backward - Span Length #>                        DIGIF                         DIGIFLEN #> 1 Digit Span Forward - Total Digit Span Forward - Span Length #>                         DIGIB                          DIGIBLEN #> 1 Digit Span Backward - Total Digit Span Backward - Span Length #>                  WAIS MINTTOTS             BOSTON        ANIMALS #> 1 WAIS-R Digit Symbol     MINT Boston Naming Test Animal Fluency #>                 VEG  UDSVERTN UDSVERFC UDSVERLC           UDSBENTC #> 1 Vegetable Fluency F+L Words  F Words  L Words Benson Figure Copy #>                                  UDSBENTD                   CRAFTVRS #> 1 Benson Delay (100% retained; Recog = 1) Craft Immediate - Verbatim #>                        LOGIMEM                    MEMUNITS #> 1 Logical Memory IA, Immediate Logical Memory IIA, Delayed #>                       CRAFTURS                              CRAFTDVR #> 1 Craft Immediate - Paraphrase Craft Delay - Verbatim (91% retained) #>                                  CRAFTDRE                              REYTOTAL #> 1 Craft Delay - Paraphrase (65% retained) RAVLT Total Learning (-4,-4,-4,-4,-4) #>                REYDLIST           REY6REC          REYDREC #> 1 RAVLT Distractor List RAVLT Short Delay RAVLT Long Delay #>                                         REYAREC #> 1 RAVLT Recognition (Correct: -4/15, FP: -4/15) #>                                    TRAILB          MOCACLOCK #> 1 Trailmaking Part B (2 errors; title: ntrd 0.3.2
text: Longitudinal table now shows raw scores color coding corresponding standardized scores Changed default methods use updated regression models also adjusts race CES-D total score included assessment summary table Mood section

title: ntrd 0.3.1
text: Bug fixes: Fixed issues wrong api key biomarker table (WADRC specific)

title: ntrd 0.3.0
text: Data sources can now saved encrypted file user specified password. Note: password saved anywhere, forgotten, file accessible. Data sources can removed added. Biomarker Table updated inclusion density curves (WADRC specific)

title: ntrd 0.2.0
text: Multiple data sources can added combined. Added ability pull biomarker data directly Panda database (WADRC specific). part UI handles plots now uses accordion. lets user collapse panels plots interest less clutter. UDS 2.0 data can imported displayed (WADRC specific). Added option download main assessment table. Lots bug fixes improvements UI.

title: ntrd 0.1.0
text: Initial version shared WADRC
